commands.author: xydt-tanshanshan

target.title: fix(agents): add tool-activity heartbeat to keep subagent alive during tool calls
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Subagent sessions are killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout while performing long-running tool calls, even though the subagent is actively collecting data and within its total `runTimeoutSeconds` budget. The LLM idle watchdog resets only on token arrival â€” tool completions do not signal it.

## Summary

- **Problem**: The LLM idle-timeout watchdog (`streamWithIdleTimeout`) only resets on LLM token arrival. During subagent tool calls (web_fetch, read, exec, etc.), the LLM stream is idle, so a subagent performing multi-minute data collection is killed at 120s despite being well within its 900s total run budget.
- **Solution**: Implement a per-run tool-activity heartbeat:
  1. `notifyToolActivity(runId)` â€” published from embedded-runner tool wrappers whenever a tool completes (success + error)
  2. `onToolActivity(runId, listener)` â€” consumed by `streamWithIdleTimeout` to reset idle watchdog on tool completion
  3. `getLastToolActivityMs(runId)` â€” shared timestamp Map, visible to `armTimer` before first stream iteration
  4. `clearToolActivityRun(runId)` â€” wired into run lifecycle `finally` block (success, error, abort, cancel, restart)
- **What changed**: 6 files (+238/-7)
    - `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) â€” per-run Maps for listeners + timestamps + cleanup
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) â€” barrel re-export
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts` (new) â€” 7 tests (pub/sub, per-run scoping, cleanup)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts` â€” accept `runId`, use `getLastToolActivityMs`, heartbeat subscription, stale timestamp guard
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` â€” 2 heartbeat integration tests
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` â€” wrap `effectiveTools` execute with `notifyToolActivity`, `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally`
- **What did NOT change**: No config changes, no provider/channel/UI changes. Idle timeout default unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#94124](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94124)

## Motivation

When a subagent performs tool calls that take minutes, the LLM stream is idle. The 120s idle timeout kills the session even though the subagent is actively gathering data. The per-run scoping prevents concurrent runs from resetting each other's idle watchdogs, and the lifecycle cleanup prevents memory leaks in long-running gateways.

ClawSweeper-identified defects addressed across iterations:
1. **Unused `unsubscribeToolActivity`** (lint) â€” removed binding
2. **Process-global heartbeat scope** â€” replaced with per-run `Map<runId>` scoping
3. **Missing lifecycle cleanup** â€” `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally` block
4. **Stale timestamp 1ms watchdog** â€” `recentActivity` guard filters timestamps older than `timeoutMs`

## Evidence

### Unit tests (85/85 passing)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts --run
 Tests  7 passed (7)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts --run
 Tests  78 passed (78)
```

### Live subagent run

- **Environment**: Linux x86_64, Node 22.19+, OpenClaw 2026.6.9, branch `fix/subagent-tool-heartbeat-94124`
- **Command**:

```bash
pnpm openclaw agent --agent main \
  --message "Use subagent to spawn a child task. The child agent should use write_file to write a timestamp and 'Hello from subagent proof test' to /tmp/subagent-proof.txt"
```

- **Output**:

```
Task completed successfully. /tmp/subagent-proof.txt written.
[agent] run 242d29b1-... ended with stopReason=stop
```

```console
$ cat /tmp/subagent-proof.txt
Hello from subagent proof at 2026-06-22 08:55:00 CST (+0800)
```

### Evidence matrix

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| Subagent dispatched and ran | âś… |
| Tool (`write_file`) executed by subagent | âś… |
| Output file created with correct content | âś… |
| `LLM idle timeout` error in output | âťŚ Not present |
| `stopReason` | `stop` (normal completion) |
| `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally` block | âś… `attempt.ts:5773` |
| Stale timestamp > timeout â†’ ignored | âś… `recentActivity` guard |
| Concurrent run isolation | âś… `Map<runId>` scoping |

- **What was not tested**: Multi-minute long-running tool call with `web_fetch` or `exec` â€” the `write_file` tool path exercises the same heartbeat code path. A >120s exec call would require a model that reliably dispatches exec within subagents.

## Root Cause

The LLM idle timeout watchdog in `streamWithIdleTimeout` observed only LLM request activity. No tool-completion path reset the watchdog, so subagents performing tool work were killed at 120s.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Subagents executing long-running tool calls will no longer be killed mid-execution with "LLM idle timeout (120s): no response from model".

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Unit test pub/sub correctness, per-run scoping, cleanup removal, timer reset on tool activity, cross-turn persistence, stale timestamp filtering
- **Edge cases checked**: No prior tool activity (full timeout), tool activity before stream creation (effective timeout), multiple resets within same stream, stale timestamp from previous stream
- **What you did NOT verify**: Multi-minute long-running tool call with `web_fetch` or `exec`

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes â€” no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Per-run Maps for listener/timestamp state, dual-level subscription (wrapper for cross-turn persistence, per-stream for immediate reset). All tool paths covered at `effectiveTools` assembly. Lifecycle cleanup via `finally` block.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: PR #94133 uses signal-keyed WeakMap with mismatched signals, only covers cataloged tools, lacks cross-turn persistence and lifecycle cleanup.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The heartbeat polling in `attempt.ts` wraps every tool execute function. Overhead is a synchronous function call per tool invocation â€” negligible.
- **Mitigation**: `clearToolActivityRun(params.runId)` in the run lifecycle `finally` block ensures Maps do not retain entries for completed runs.
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
